person.name: Jen Weigel
person.role: Program manager and chief-of-staff style operator for AI enablement
person.goal: Understand my work and find AI leverage

summary.jobInPlainEnglish: I run a portfolio of AI enablement and transformation work that depends on synthesis, follow-through, and structured operating cadence. My time goes into collecting uneven inputs from many owners, turning them into useful views for leadership, and keeping cross-workstream commitments moving. The recurring work is highly pattern-based, but the judgment about what matters, what to escalate, and how to frame tradeoffs stays with me.
summary.accountabilityAndOutcomes: I am accountable for portfolio clarity, follow-through across multiple workstreams, and leadership-ready narratives about progress, blockers, and opportunities.
summary.workRhythms: My work is shaped by weekly standups, monthly aggregation cycles, recurring stakeholder updates, and ad hoc escalations when signals change.
summary.recurringArtifacts: I repeatedly create responsibility maps, status briefs, standup pre-reads, narrative updates, action-item trackers, and operating-model drafts.
summary.frictionMap: The drag comes from inconsistent updates, context switching across programs, having to restitch the same story from multiple sources, and manually tracking follow-through across owners.
summary.humanOnlyWork: I keep the final tradeoff calls, relationship management, escalation timing, leadership messaging, and design judgment for new models or frameworks.
summary.aiLeveragePoints: AI should do the first-pass aggregation, drafting, pattern detection, and workflow tracking. It should assist rather than decide when the work depends on persuasion, strategic tradeoffs, or organizational taste.
topRecurringPatterns: Turn scattered inputs into decision-ready artifacts
Monitor for drift, blockers, and stale commitments
Prepare for recurring meetings and operating cadences

responsibilities.title: KR 3.1 portfolio integration
responsibilities.outcome: Clear, current portfolio view with blockers, support needs, and credible impact signals.
responsibilities.humanRoleLeft: Challenge weak logic, make tradeoffs, and approve external framing.
responsibilities.notes: Strong candidate for aggregation and monitoring assistance.

responsibilities.title: Agentic Audience standup
responsibilities.outcome: Cross-workstream progress is visible and blockers surface before they stall momentum.
responsibilities.humanRoleLeft: Facilitate the live conversation and decide where to push.
responsibilities.notes: Ideal for pre-read assembly and post-meeting carry-forward.

responsibilities.title: Monthly AI transformation narrative
responsibilities.outcome: Leadership gets a crisp story about progress and the behaviors worth reinforcing.
responsibilities.humanRoleLeft: Own final narrative framing, audience judgment, and final polish.
responsibilities.notes: High-value drafting support, but never fully autonomous.

responsibilities.title: Cohort-based AI acceleration design
responsibilities.outcome: A repeatable model exists for helping teams adopt AI in a structured way.
responsibilities.cadence: Design phase with ad hoc working sessions
painPoints: Need to balance breadth versus depth
responsibilities.humanRoleLeft: Keep strategic design, selection criteria, and readiness calls human-led.

responsibilities.title: Task and accountability operations
responsibilities.outcome: Nothing falls through the cracks and follow-through is visible across the portfolio.
responsibilities.humanRoleLeft: Approve creation, prioritize, and handle sensitive escalation moments.
responsibilities.notes: Best end-to-end automation candidate in the set.

responsibilities.title: Daily ops and triage
responsibilities.outcome: The day starts with a tight view of what matters, what changed, and what needs attention.
responsibilities.humanRoleLeft: Decide what to act on and what to ignore after the digest surfaces it.
responsibilities.notes: Good fit for monitoring and summarization.
